SEC EDGAR XBRL Excel Add-in — Installation Guide
=================================================

IMPORTANT: Follow every step in order. Skipping any step is the reason
the ribbon tab does not appear or Excel says "cannot access the file."

------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 1 — Save the file to your Documents folder
------------------------------------------------------------------

Download SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam and save it here:

    C:\Users\YourName\Documents\SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam

Do NOT save it to:
  x  Your Desktop
  x  OneDrive or any synced folder
  x  The root of C:\ (e.g. C:\SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam)
  x  Any network or USB drive

Only a plain local folder like Documents will work.

------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 2 — Unblock the file (REQUIRED after every download)
------------------------------------------------------------------

Windows marks every downloaded file as "untrusted." You must
manually unblock it or Excel will refuse to run it.

  1. Open File Explorer and navigate to your Documents folder
  2. Right-click on SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam
  3. Click Properties
  4. At the very bottom of the General tab, look for a line that
     says "Security: This file came from another computer..."
  5. Check the box next to "Unblock"
  6. Click OK

If you do not see an "Unblock" checkbox, the file is already
trusted and you can skip to Step 3.

------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 3 — Add your Documents folder as a Trusted Location
------------------------------------------------------------------

Excel requires the folder containing the add-in to be explicitly
trusted, or it will load silently without the ribbon tab.

  1. Open Excel (do not open any file, just the blank Excel window)
  2. Click File → Options
  3. Click "Trust Center" in the left sidebar
  4. Click "Trust Center Settings..." button on the right
  5. Click "Trusted Locations" in the left sidebar
  6. Click "Add new location..."
  7. Click "Browse..." and navigate to your Documents folder
     (C:\Users\YourName\Documents)
  8. Click OK → OK → OK to close all dialogs

------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 4 — Install the add-in
------------------------------------------------------------------

  1. In Excel, click File → Options
  2. Click "Add-ins" in the left sidebar
  3. At the bottom, the "Manage:" dropdown must say "Excel Add-ins"
     (NOT "COM Add-ins", NOT "Automation" — those show .dll files
     and will not work)
  4. Click "Go..."
  5. Click "Browse..."
  6. Navigate to Documents and select SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam
  7. Click OK
  8. Make sure the checkbox next to "SEC XBRL Addin" is checked
  9. Click OK

------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 5 — Enable Content (if prompted)
------------------------------------------------------------------

If Excel shows a yellow bar saying "Macros have been disabled",
click Enable Content.

This bar only appears if you skipped Step 3. Adding your Documents
folder as a Trusted Location (Step 3) eliminates this prompt.

------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 6 — Confirm it worked
------------------------------------------------------------------

A new tab called "SEC EDGAR" will appear in the Excel ribbon.

Click it, then click "Pull SEC Financials", type a ticker like
AAPL or MSFT, and press Enter.

------------------------------------------------------------------
TROUBLESHOOTING
------------------------------------------------------------------

Problem: "Microsoft Excel cannot access the file"
Fix: The file is in a bad location (Desktop, OneDrive, C:\ root,
     or a network drive). Move it to Documents and repeat Step 4.

Problem: "This file type is not supported in Protected View"
Fix: You skipped Step 2. Right-click the file → Properties →
     check Unblock → OK. Then repeat Step 4.

Problem: The "SEC EDGAR" tab does not appear after installing
Fix: You skipped Step 3. Add your Documents folder as a Trusted
     Location, then close and reopen Excel.

Problem: The checkbox in Step 4 is greyed out / won't stay checked
Fix: Close Excel completely. Reopen it WITHOUT opening any file.
     Then repeat Step 4.

Problem: Yellow bar says "Macros have been disabled"
Fix: Click Enable Content. To make this permanent, add your
     Documents folder as a Trusted Location (Step 3).

------------------------------------------------------------------
UNINSTALLING
------------------------------------------------------------------

  1. File → Options → Add-ins → Manage: Excel Add-ins → Go
  2. Uncheck "SEC XBRL Addin" → OK
  3. Delete SEC_XBRL_Addin.xlam from your Documents folder
